_comment: Capability profile for the dock body. See DESIGN.md §2.
2026-05-27 — protocol redesign. The body now advertises parts +
primitive parameter ranges only. Named states (lookUp, forward, ...)
live in the brain's state catalog (e.g. app/src/main/assets/states.json).
For v0 smoke firmware (dock_body_v0): only `neck` and `foot` are wired.
`arm.left` and `arm.right` are sketched here so the brain knows what
additional parts will appear when hardware lands; the smoke firmware
Pulse-width-µs ranges are the MG90S clone safe span (500-2500). Real
mechanical end-stops may be narrower; brain's state catalog SHOULD
stay inside [800, 2200] to avoid stall buzz at extremes.
duration_ms.range = [0, 30000]: 0 = snap, 30s upper bound is policy
(no upper limit on the wire; we just don't expect anything slower).
velocity_us_per_sec_cap: MG90S spec is ~10000 us/s peak; we cap at
4000 to keep wear down and motion smooth.
name: dock-body-smoke-v0

neck.description: Single-DOF pitch servo on the head — nods up/down. MG90S clone.

pulse_width_us.description: Servo PWM pulse width. 1500 µs is mechanical center.

duration_ms.description: Linear-interpolation time to reach target pulse_width_us. 0 = snap.

velocity_us_per_sec_cap.description: Max rate of change. 0 = use device default. Brain must not exceed declared cap.

foot.description: Yaw servo at the base — turns the dock around its vertical axis.

pulse_width_us.description: Servo PWM pulse width. 1500 µs is mechanical center.

duration_ms.description: Linear-interpolation time to reach target pulse_width_us. 0 = snap.

velocity_us_per_sec_cap.description: Max rate of change. 0 = use device default.
